methodology_sections: 2.3 Data Flow Tracing - Input flow, external data, asset movement, permission escalation
3.2 Business Logic Analysis - Economic incentives, edge cases, assumption violations, race conditions

path: Funds deployed via Connectors to DeFi protocols
transformations: base token -> vault shares via share price calculation
data_flows.trust_changes: User funds transition from self-custody to vault management, then to external protocol custody
exposure_risks: Stale TVL data leading to incorrect share price
manipulation_risks: Share price manipulation via first depositor attack
TVL inflation through connector TVL miscalculation

path: Strategy Manager submits encrypted tx via IPFS
data_flows.trust_changes: Encrypted data becomes executable transactions; multi-sig validation required
exposure_risks: Keepers have broad execution authority over all connectors
manipulation_risks: Gas limit parameter could cause selective execution failures

path: LZHelperSender.updateTVL encodes and sends via LayerZero
Position updated in Registry with TVL and timestamp
transformations: Connector TVLs aggregated into single value
TVL value encoded as bytes for cross-chain message
Decoded on base chain and stored as holding position
data_flows.trust_changes: On-chain computed value transmitted through LayerZero messaging layer - trust in LZ delivery
exposure_risks: Stale TVL data if messages are delayed or reordered
manipulation_risks: Old TVL message replayed if LZ delivers out of order
No monotonic timestamp check in updateTVL allows stale data to overwrite fresh data

path: Access control branch: accountingManager, active connector, flashLoan, or swap route
transformations: Amount potentially modified by Watcher verification (newAmount from decoded data)
data_flows.trust_changes: Tokens move from connector custody to trusted recipient; Watcher validation provides an additional check layer
exposure_risks: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is a no-op, providing zero protection
FlashLoan contract can extract tokens from any connector
manipulation_risks: Since verifyRemoveLiquidity does nothing, any withdrawal amount passes
Flash loan path bypasses watcher verification entirely

path: holdingPositionId computed as keccak256(msg.sender, positionId, data)
For removal: swap-and-pop with last element
transformations: Position data encoded into holding position struct
data_flows.trust_changes: Only enabled connectors can modify positions
manipulation_risks: Key mismatch between creation (uses msg.sender) and swap reconstruction (uses calculatorConnector) causes mapping corruption for token positions

path: makeFlashLoan stores caller and triggers Balancer vault flash loan
receiveFlashLoan validates msg.sender == vault and caller == keeperContract
Arbitrary calls executed to destinationConnector[] with callingData[]
transformations: Flash loaned tokens deployed temporarily to connector, then returned with fees
sinks: Arbitrary destination contracts via destinationConnector calls
data_flows.trust_changes: Keeper trust extended to arbitrary contract calls; destinationConnector not validated against registry
exposure_risks: destinationConnector addresses not validated - arbitrary contract calls possible
caller stored in state (not transient) during execution
manipulation_risks: Keeper can use flash loans to call any contract with any calldata
No validation that destinationConnectors are registry-approved

path: Eligible user calls executeSwap with SwapRequest
Slippage calculated from oracle if checkForSlippage is true
Swap implementation (e.g., LiFi) executes the swap
transformations: Input token -> output token via external DEX
Slippage bounds computed from oracle price
data_flows.trust_changes: Tokens temporarily trusted to LiFi router contract
validation_points: Route existence (BUGGY - uses AND instead of OR)
exposure_risks: Disabled routes with valid addresses still pass modifier check
Oracle price manipulation affects slippage calculation
manipulation_risks: onlyExistingRoute modifier allows disabled routes to pass if route address is non-zero

core_rules: Only enabled connectors can interact with vault positions
Trusted positions must use trusted tokens from connector or accounting manager
Holding positions are bounded by maxNumHoldingPositions (default 20)
Bridge transactions require two-step approval with 30-minute waiting period
Health factor must stay above minimumHealthFactor for borrowing connectors
Multi-sig threshold must be > 1 and <= numOwners
Position index 0 is reserved as a dummy placeholder
economic_incentives: Withdrawal fees charged on instant withdrawals
Management fees charged over time on AUM
Performance fees charged on profit generation
Keeper operators incentivized to execute strategies correctly via multi-sig
Watchers incentivized to monitor and revert suspicious actions
governance_mechanisms: Registry GOVERNER_ROLE is admin for all roles
Vault-level governance: governer, maintainer, maintainerWithoutTimeLock, keeper, watcher, emergency
Keepers multi-sig requires threshold-of-n signatures with ascending address ordering
Bridge transaction requires pre-approval + time delay

edge_cases.scenario: Registry onlyVaultMaintainer modifier uses OR instead of AND for access control
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Either vault maintainer OR EMERGENCY_ROLE holder should be able to call protected functions
edge_cases.actual_behavior: ONLY addresses that are BOTH the vault maintainer AND hold EMERGENCY_ROLE can call. Regular maintainer without EMERGENCY_ROLE is locked out
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Core governance functions (addConnector, updateConnectorTrustedTokens, removeTrustedPosition, changeVaultAddresses) become inaccessible to their intended callers. Protocol cannot be properly administered. Same bug in onlyVaultMaintainerWithoutTimeLock (affects addTrustedPosition) and onlyVaultGoverner (affects changeVaultAddresses)

edge_cases.scenario: Holding position swap-and-pop uses wrong key for isPositionUsed mapping reconstruction
edge_cases.expected_behavior: When removing a position and swapping with the last element, the moved element's isPositionUsed key should match the key used during creation
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Creation uses keccak256(msg.sender, positionId, data) but swap reconstruction uses keccak256(calculatorConnector, positionId, data). For token positions (type 0), calculatorConnector is accountingManager while msg.sender is the connector - these differ
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: isPositionUsed mapping becomes corrupted for token positions after any removal that triggers swap-and-pop. Subsequent lookups return wrong indices. Positions may become unremovable or duplicate entries created, eventually exhausting maxNumHoldingPositions

edge_cases.scenario: UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL queries pool-level aggregated position instead of per-NFT position
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL should reflect only this vault's liquidity in the Uniswap V3 position
edge_cases.actual_behavior: pool.positions(key) returns ALL liquidity from the NonfungiblePositionManager at that tick range, including positions from other users/vaults. TVL is overcounted
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Inflated TVL leads to inflated share price. Existing depositors can withdraw more than their fair share. An attacker could add liquidity in the same pool/tick range through the same positionManager to inflate the vault's apparent TVL

edge_cases.scenario: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function providing no validation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Function should verify that withdrawal amounts are legitimate and within bounds
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Function body is empty - any withdrawal amount passes verification
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: The Watcher verification step in BaseConnector.sendTokensToTrustedAddress provides zero protection. A compromised keeper could extract any amount from connectors through the accountingManager path without meaningful checks

edge_cases.scenario: SwapAndBridgeHandler.onlyExistingRoute modifier uses AND instead of OR for validation
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Route should be rejected if address is zero OR if route is disabled
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Route is only rejected if address is zero AND route is disabled. A disabled route with a non-zero address passes the check. A route with address(0) but enabled=true also passes
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Disabled swap/bridge routes can still be used for transactions, bypassing the disabling mechanism. This could allow use of compromised or deprecated routes

edge_cases.scenario: Multiple connector TVL calculations can underflow (Silo, Prisma, Dolomite, Compound, Aave)
edge_cases.expected_behavior: When debt exceeds collateral, TVL should return 0 gracefully
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Subtraction of debt from collateral in _getPositionTVL reverts due to Solidity 0.8.x underflow protection, causing entire TVL calculation to fail
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If any position becomes undercollateralized (e.g., during market volatility or liquidation), the entire vault's TVL calculation fails. This blocks all deposits and withdrawals, effectively DoS-ing the vault

edge_cases.scenario: DolomiteConnector.openBorrowPosition passes removePosition=true instead of false
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Opening a borrow position should ADD the position to the registry
edge_cases.actual_behavior: The function calls registry.updateHoldingPosition with removePosition=true, attempting to REMOVE a position that was just created
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Borrow positions are not properly tracked in the registry. TVL calculations will miss these positions, leading to inaccurate accounting. The position data (accountId) is lost from tracking

edge_cases.scenario: StargateConnector casts poolId from uint256 to uint16
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Pool ID should be preserved accurately across all function calls
edge_cases.actual_behavior: stargateRouter.instantRedeemLocal casts uint256 poolId to uint16, truncating values > 65535
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If a pool has an ID larger than 65535, the withdrawal would target a completely different pool, potentially losing funds

edge_cases.scenario: Cross-chain TVL update has no timestamp monotonicity check
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only newer TVL updates should overwrite older ones
edge_cases.actual_behavior: OmnichainManagerBaseChain.updateTVL accepts any updateTime without checking if it's newer than the existing timestamp
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If LayerZero delivers messages out of order, a stale TVL value could overwrite a more recent one, causing temporary share price miscalculation

edge_cases.scenario: SiloConnector._getPositionTVL assumes all tokens have 18 decimals
edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL calculation should account for different token decimal scales
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses _getValue(asset, base, 1e18) as a price per 1e18 units, then multiplies by balance and divides by 1e18. For tokens with non-18 decimals (e.g., USDC with 6), this produces incorrect values
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Incorrect TVL for positions involving non-18-decimal tokens leads to wrong share prices and enables depositors to extract more value than entitled

edge_cases.expected_behavior: All state-changing functions should be protected against reentrancy
edge_cases.actual_behavior: repayWithCollateral has no nonReentrant modifier, unlike all other Aave connector functions
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Potential reentrancy via Aave callback if the repayment token has hooks. Limited by onlyManager access control

edge_cases.scenario: BalancerFlashLoan.receiveFlashLoan does not validate destinationConnector addresses
edge_cases.expected_behavior: All call targets should be validated against the registry as active connectors
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Only the receiver address is checked against registry. destinationConnector[] targets are called without any validation
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Keeper (via multi-sig) can use flash loans to make arbitrary calls to any contract with any calldata, potentially bypassing connector-specific safety checks

edge_cases.scenario: LZHelperSender.updateTVL uses entire contract balance as messaging fee
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Only the required fee amount should be paid for the LayerZero message
edge_cases.actual_behavior: MessagingFee(address(this).balance, 0) sends ALL ETH in the contract as the native fee
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Anyone who sends ETH to the LZHelperSender contract loses it to LayerZero fees. If too little ETH is present, messages may fail

edge_cases.expected_behavior: TVL should accurately reflect the net position value
edge_cases.actual_behavior: TVL = _getValue(collateralType, base, totalDeposited + totalAssigned). totalDeposited includes totalAssigned and totalLocked. Adding totalAssigned again double-counts delegated collateral
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: Inflated TVL for SNX positions leads to higher share prices, enabling value extraction by existing depositors

edge_cases.scenario: GearboxV3._getPositionTVL uses address(840) as token address for USD denomination
edge_cases.expected_behavior: Value oracle should correctly convert from USD to base token
edge_cases.actual_behavior: Uses _getValue(address(840), base, amount) where address(840) is a magic number for USD. If valueOracle doesn't have a price for this address, the call reverts
edge_cases.exploitation_potential: If the oracle configuration doesn't include the address(840) -> base token price feed, Gearbox positions break TVL calculation, potentially DoS-ing the vault

assumption_violations: Registry modifiers assume OR logic but implement AND logic - fundamental access control assumption violated
Position key reconstruction assumes calculatorConnector == creating connector - violated for all token positions (type 0)
UNIv3 TVL calculation assumes pool positions are per-owner - violated as NonfungiblePositionManager aggregates positions across all users
Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity assumes it provides security check - violated as function body is empty
onlyExistingRoute modifier assumes disabled routes are blocked - violated due to wrong boolean logic
Connector TVL calculations assume collateral always >= debt - violated during market stress or liquidation events
SiloConnector assumes 18 decimal tokens for price normalization - violated for USDC, USDT, WBTC etc.
Cross-chain TVL sync assumes message ordering - violated if LZ delivers out of order
game_theory_risks: TVL overcounting in UNIv3 can be gamed by adding liquidity to same pool/tick range to inflate vault share price
Empty watcher verification allows unrestricted fund extraction through keeper operations
Disabled routes remain usable due to modifier bug, creating opportunity to use deprecated/compromised routes
Cross-chain TVL staleness can be exploited: deposit when stale TVL is low (cheap shares), trigger TVL update, withdraw at higher price
Flash loan mechanism allows keeper multi-sig to make arbitrary external calls, expanding attack surface beyond defined connector operations
integration_risks: LayerZero message delivery ordering not guaranteed, affecting TVL sync accuracy
LiFi router integration assumes correct extractGenericSwapParameters implementation
Balancer flash loan fees assumed to be zero - if Balancer governance changes fee structure, operations may fail
Aave getUserAccountData denomination assumptions may break across different Aave deployments
Stargate poolId uint16 truncation may cause wrong pool interactions on certain deployments
Compound V3 UserBasic struct field interpretation depends on specific Compound version
calculation_errors: SiloConnector._getPositionTVL: price normalization uses 1e18 base regardless of token decimals - produces incorrect values for non-18-decimal tokens
UNIv3Connector._getPositionTVL: pool.positions(key) returns aggregated liquidity across ALL NFTs in that tick range, not just the vault's position
SNXv3Connector._getPositionTVL: totalDeposited + totalAssigned likely double-counts since totalDeposited typically includes totalAssigned
Multiple connectors (Silo, Prisma, Dolomite, Compound, Aave): collateral - debt subtraction can underflow reverting entire TVL calculation
DolomiteConnector.openBorrowPosition: passes true (remove) instead of false (add) to registry, losing position tracking

token_semantics.notes: Lido stETH is a rebasing token. LidoConnector stores amount at request time in additionalData - cached value may drift from actual rebased balance. 1-2 wei rounding on transfers.
Non-standard ERC20 approval. SafeERC20 and forceApprove used correctly in _approveOperations. SiloConnector price calculation assumes 18 decimals - INCORRECT for USDT.
6-decimal token. SiloConnector._getPositionTVL price normalization with 1e18 base produces wrong values.
Used in LidoConnector for ETH wrapping/unwrapping. Standard behavior.
LP tokens representing liquidity in Stargate pools. amountLPtoLD conversion used for TVL calculation.
ERC721 position token. ERC721Holder implemented in UNIv3Connector and PancakeswapConnector.
Magic address used in GearboxV3 to represent USD. Requires oracle support for address(840) -> baseToken price feed.

invariants.name: holding_position_index_consistency
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: isPositionUsed key uses msg.sender at creation but calculatorConnector during swap-and-pop removal. Keys diverge for token positions (type 0) where calculatorConnector != calling connector.

invariants.name: tvl_non_negative
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: Multiple connectors (Silo, Prisma, Dolomite, Compound, Aave) perform unchecked subtraction of debt from collateral, reverting on underflow instead of returning 0.

invariants.name: access_control_or_semantics
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: Registry modifiers use || (OR) in revert condition requiring BOTH roles instead of EITHER. Should use && (AND) in revert condition.

invariants.name: route_disabled_means_unusable
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: onlyExistingRoute uses AND in condition, allowing disabled routes with non-zero addresses to pass.

invariants.name: position_tvl_accuracy
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: UNIv3Connector queries aggregated pool position instead of individual NFT position, overcounting TVL.

invariants.name: watcher_verification_provides_security
invariants.notes: VIOLATED: Watchers.verifyRemoveLiquidity is an empty function body - no verification performed.
